market_overview.headline: Market structure deteriorated with SPY bearish unstacked and QQQ in full bear mode amid broad selling pressure.
market_overview.detail: 67% of stocks now in bearish signals vs only 37% bullish, with tech leading the decline. Major indices all trading below key EMAs with potential bullish crosses offering only modest hope.

reversal_candidates.headline: Early Reversal Signals: Bear Rallies with Bullish Cross Potential

items.name: Netflix, Inc.
items.note: Price action above 13W EMA with pending bullish crossover after price hike announcement suggests potential trend reversal.

items.note: Semiconductor leader with $1.5T market cap showing bullish cross potential amid memory chip sector weakness.

items.note: EV leader showing potential bullish cross setup despite broader consumer durables weakness.

items.note: Storage tech leader with +11% above 21W EMA, normal volume suggests healthy pullback for entry.

items.note: Semiconductor equipment giant holding +10% above 21W despite memory chip sector headwinds.

items.name: Lockheed Martin Corporation
items.note: Defense contractor with low volume pullback suggests orderly selling, ideal for swing entry amid geopolitical tensions.

items.name: FedEx Corporation
items.note: Transportation leader showing low volume pullback in established uptrend, quality setup for logistics play.

items.name: Walmart Inc.
items.note: Retail giant with CEO succession news, low volume pullback offers defensive consumer exposure entry.

momentum_themes.headline: Momentum Concentrated in Storage & Optical Technology
momentum_themes.detail: Storage technology leading with SNDK and WDC showing exceptional spread scores, while optical components LITE and CIEN accelerating. Energy services and defense tech also showing relative strength.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance vs Tech/Finance Weakness

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score with all 15 names in Full Bull structure, benefiting from geopolitical tensions.
Small but strong sector with all names bullish amid broader market weakness.
Infrastructure and services showing resilience with strong bullish participation.

weakest.note: Complete bearish capitulation across all commercial services names.
Tech services decimated with 91% bearish, major reversal candidates emerging.
Healthcare services under pressure with 75% bearish participation.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear risk-off rotation from growth/tech into defensive energy and industrials. Finance sector -62% net score reflects rate/credit concerns.

items.text: Memory chip stocks hit hard for second consecutive day with elevated volume - sector breakdown accelerating
SPY and QQQ both below key EMAs with crossover alerts offering only modest bullish potential
BTC deep bear structure (-47% from fair value) suggests crypto correlation risk persists
Iran tensions escalating - watch for energy spike/travel disruption amplifying market stress

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Geopolitical tensions with Iran dominated headlines as memory chip stocks extended losses for the second day. Netflix price hikes and United Airlines labor deal provided sector-specific catalysts amid broader market weakness driven by DHS shutdown concerns.

items.text: Iran war tensions cooling tourist demand while hardliners call for nuclear weapons - energy/travel sector impacts
Memory chip stocks hit hard second consecutive day with elevated selling pressure
Netflix raises prices across all streaming plans, stock rallies on pricing power demonstration
Trump threatens 'drastic measures' over DHS shutdown as TSA delays mount nationwide
Major CEOs citing AI disruption as factor in stepping down - Coca-Cola, Walmart leadership changes
